acceptanceCriteria: Command Central has a dedicated Linear project for health/orchestration issues instead of mixing them into Config.
Imported issues preserve bracketed CC-* keys for idempotent updates.
The project explicitly uses Symphony vocabulary as a projection shape, not a replacement scheduler.
Cross-project issues can be grouped, reviewed, and advanced from this tracker without losing source authority boundaries.
items.body:
## Problem
Create a dedicated Command Central Linear project/workstream for issues like the false-looking OpenClaw DOWN indicator and for orchestration work that spans OpenClaw, TaskFlow, Launcher, Linear, and the Symphony-shaped Command Central surface. Command Central remains the visibility/control plane; OpenClaw/TaskFlow/Launcher remain lifecycle authority.

## Evidence
- Source repo: `/Users/ostehost/projects/command-central`
- Source HEAD: `257aadc4`
- Handoff key: `CC-000`

## Scope
- Lane: `Program`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Command Central has a dedicated Linear project for health/orchestration issues instead of mixing them into Config.
- Imported issues preserve bracketed CC-* keys for idempotent updates.
- The project explicitly uses Symphony vocabulary as a projection shape, not a replacement scheduler.
- Cross-project issues can be grouped, reviewed, and advanced from this tracker without losing source authority boundaries.

## Verification
- `memory_search: Command Central Symphony spec Linear project orchestration across projects OpenClaw Down status bar`: Found Symphony adoption path: CC projects OpenClaw/TaskFlow/Launcher records into Symphony/Codex Runs; OpenClaw remains lifecycle authority.
- `Linear read-only list teams/projects`: Workspace has team PartnerAI (PAR), existing projects Config and Local Directory Sites; no Command Central project detected.
- `git rev-parse --short HEAD`: 257aadc4

## Dependencies / Blocks
- None

## Out Of Scope
- Superseded findings are context only and must not be re-opened by this issue.

<!-- openclaw-linear-intake:v1 -->

acceptanceCriteria: Reproduce or explain the OpenClaw DOWN + active-task-count state from a real Command Central session.
Identify whether the failing source is gateway reachability, channel health, ACP/task service, node host, cached state, or status-bar composition.
Update the health model so degraded/partial states are not collapsed into a misleading all-down label.
Add tests covering down, degraded, stale-cache, and task-service-alive combinations.
Verify in VS Code or an equivalent extension host that the status bar renders the corrected label/state.
items.body:
## Problem
The Command Central status bar can show a red 'OpenClaw DOWN' state while other evidence indicates OpenClaw still has active task state ('1 working · 3 done'). This looks like a broken feature or stale/partial health calculation. Diagnose the source of truth and make the UI truthful.

## Evidence
- Source repo: `/Users/ostehost/projects/command-central`
- Source HEAD: `257aadc4`
- Handoff key: `CC-001`

## Scope
- Lane: `Health`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Reproduce or explain the OpenClaw DOWN + active-task-count state from a real Command Central session.
- Identify whether the failing source is gateway reachability, channel health, ACP/task service, node host, cached state, or status-bar composition.
- Update the health model so degraded/partial states are not collapsed into a misleading all-down label.
- Add tests covering down, degraded, stale-cache, and task-service-alive combinations.
- Verify in VS Code or an equivalent extension host that the status bar renders the corrected label/state.

## Verification
- `memory_search: Command Central Symphony spec Linear project orchestration across projects OpenClaw Down status bar`: Found Symphony adoption path: CC projects OpenClaw/TaskFlow/Launcher records into Symphony/Codex Runs; OpenClaw remains lifecycle authority.
- `Linear read-only list teams/projects`: Workspace has team PartnerAI (PAR), existing projects Config and Local Directory Sites; no Command Central project detected.
- `git rev-parse --short HEAD`: 257aadc4

## Dependencies / Blocks
- None

## Out Of Scope
- Superseded findings are context only and must not be re-opened by this issue.

<!-- openclaw-linear-intake:v1 -->

acceptanceCriteria: Health dimensions and precedence are documented in Command Central research or source docs.
The status provider distinguishes unreachable, auth failed, stale, degraded, and healthy states.
Task counts are explicitly tied to the task source that supplied them and do not imply gateway health by accident.
Tests assert precedence and labels for mixed-health states.
items.body:
## Problem
Document and implement a health contract that separates gateway process status, API reachability, channel health, TaskFlow/task availability, node connectivity, and launcher execution health. The UI should show precise degraded states instead of one ambiguous DOWN banner.

## Evidence
- Source repo: `/Users/ostehost/projects/command-central`
- Source HEAD: `257aadc4`
- Handoff key: `CC-002`

## Scope
- Lane: `Architecture`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Health dimensions and precedence are documented in Command Central research or source docs.
- The status provider distinguishes unreachable, auth failed, stale, degraded, and healthy states.
- Task counts are explicitly tied to the task source that supplied them and do not imply gateway health by accident.
- Tests assert precedence and labels for mixed-health states.

## Verification
- `memory_search: Command Central Symphony spec Linear project orchestration across projects OpenClaw Down status bar`: Found Symphony adoption path: CC projects OpenClaw/TaskFlow/Launcher records into Symphony/Codex Runs; OpenClaw remains lifecycle authority.
- `Linear read-only list teams/projects`: Workspace has team PartnerAI (PAR), existing projects Config and Local Directory Sites; no Command Central project detected.
- `git rev-parse --short HEAD`: 257aadc4

## Dependencies / Blocks
- CC-001 informs the concrete failure mode and reproduction evidence.

## Out Of Scope
- Superseded findings are context only and must not be re-opened by this issue.

<!-- openclaw-linear-intake:v1 -->
dependencies: CC-001 informs the concrete failure mode and reproduction evidence.

items.body:
## Problem
Extend the Command Central Symphony/Codex Runs projection so workstreams can group records from TaskFlow, launcher tasks, and Linear issue keys without becoming a competing scheduler. This is the tracker/visibility layer for cross-project orchestration.

## Evidence
- Source repo: `/Users/ostehost/projects/command-central`
- Source HEAD: `257aadc4`
- Handoff key: `CC-003`

## Scope
- Lane: `Symphony`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Codex/Symphony run views can display a parent workstream/group with child task attempts and source refs.
- Field provenance remains visible: Linear issue, TaskFlow flow, launcher task, OpenClaw task, and workspace evidence are not merged by fuzzy title alone.
- The projection preserves the rule that OpenClaw/TaskFlow/Launcher own lifecycle state.
- Tests cover explicit-key joins and prevent broad display-title/path-only joins.

## Verification
- `memory_search: Command Central Symphony spec Linear project orchestration across projects OpenClaw Down status bar`: Found Symphony adoption path: CC projects OpenClaw/TaskFlow/Launcher records into Symphony/Codex Runs; OpenClaw remains lifecycle authority.
- `Linear read-only list teams/projects`: Workspace has team PartnerAI (PAR), existing projects Config and Local Directory Sites; no Command Central project detected.
- `git rev-parse --short HEAD`: 257aadc4

## Dependencies / Blocks
- CC-002 establishes health/source truth vocabulary used by the projection.

## Out Of Scope
- Superseded findings are context only and must not be re-opened by this issue.

<!-- openclaw-linear-intake:v1 -->
dependencies: CC-002 establishes health/source truth vocabulary used by the projection.

items.body:
## Problem
Use deterministic Linear intake artifacts and receipts for Command Central issue creation/update, then design the mirror path for status comments, task receipts, and review evidence. Linear is the tracker boundary, not the scheduler.

## Evidence
- Source repo: `/Users/ostehost/projects/command-central`
- Source HEAD: `257aadc4`
- Handoff key: `CC-004`

## Scope
- Lane: `Linear`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Command Central handoff artifacts can be converted to import plans with the openclaw-linear-intake skill.
- Dry-run receipts are saved and safe to approve before any live mutation.
- Live applies require explicit project/team destination and approved receipt.
- Mirror updates/comment behavior are specified separately from task dispatch.

## Verification
- `memory_search: Command Central Symphony spec Linear project orchestration across projects OpenClaw Down status bar`: Found Symphony adoption path: CC projects OpenClaw/TaskFlow/Launcher records into Symphony/Codex Runs; OpenClaw remains lifecycle authority.
- `Linear read-only list teams/projects`: Workspace has team PartnerAI (PAR), existing projects Config and Local Directory Sites; no Command Central project detected.
- `git rev-parse --short HEAD`: 257aadc4

## Dependencies / Blocks
- CC-000 creates the destination project/workstream.

## Out Of Scope
- Superseded findings are context only and must not be re-opened by this issue.

acceptanceCriteria: A repo-owned WORKFLOW.md or equivalent config defines tracker, eligible states, workspace root, concurrency, retry, hooks, and prompt template.
The conductor loop is specified as poll/select/launch/review/retry/update with crash-safe receipts.
No code path bypasses visible launcher lanes for implementation work.
The design names the Command Central projection artifacts needed to monitor the loop.
items.body:
## Problem
Define the missing conductor loop that reads eligible Linear/TaskFlow work, launches visible Claude lanes through Ghostty/launcher, reviews completions, retries when safe, and updates mirrors. The daemon should use Symphony run-attempt vocabulary but keep OpenClaw/TaskFlow/Launcher as lifecycle owners.

## Evidence
- Source repo: `/Users/ostehost/projects/command-central`
- Source HEAD: `257aadc4`
- Handoff key: `CC-005`

## Scope
- Lane: `Conductor`

## Acceptance Criteria
- A repo-owned WORKFLOW.md or equivalent config defines tracker, eligible states, workspace root, concurrency, retry, hooks, and prompt template.
- The conductor loop is specified as poll/select/launch/review/retry/update with crash-safe receipts.
- No code path bypasses visible launcher lanes for implementation work.
- The design names the Command Central projection artifacts needed to monitor the loop.

## Verification
- `memory_search: Command Central Symphony spec Linear project orchestration across projects OpenClaw Down status bar`: Found Symphony adoption path: CC projects OpenClaw/TaskFlow/Launcher records into Symphony/Codex Runs; OpenClaw remains lifecycle authority.
- `Linear read-only list teams/projects`: Workspace has team PartnerAI (PAR), existing projects Config and Local Directory Sites; no Command Central project detected.
- `git rev-parse --short HEAD`: 257aadc4

## Dependencies / Blocks
- CC-003 provides the visibility projection shape.
- CC-004 provides tracker intake/mirror boundaries.

## Out Of Scope
- Superseded findings are context only and must not be re-opened by this issue.

<!-- openclaw-linear-intake:v1 -->
dependencies: CC-003 provides the visibility projection shape.

items.body:
## Problem
Use the new Command Central project to run real issues like the OpenClaw DOWN indicator through the intended loop: Linear issue, TaskFlow/workstream, visible launcher lane, completion review, receipts, and Command Central/Symphony projection.

## Evidence
- Source repo: `/Users/ostehost/projects/command-central`
- Source HEAD: `257aadc4`
- Handoff key: `CC-006`

## Scope
- Lane: `Dogfood`

## Acceptance Criteria
- At least one issue is run from Linear/project intake through visible launcher execution and reviewed completion.
- Command Central shows the workstream, attempt state, health state, and evidence links accurately during the run.
- Failures or stale states become follow-up issues in the same project, not ad-hoc chat memory.
- A short dogfood report records what was fixed and what remained blocked.

## Verification
- `memory_search: Command Central Symphony spec Linear project orchestration across projects OpenClaw Down status bar`: Found Symphony adoption path: CC projects OpenClaw/TaskFlow/Launcher records into Symphony/Codex Runs; OpenClaw remains lifecycle authority.
- `Linear read-only list teams/projects`: Workspace has team PartnerAI (PAR), existing projects Config and Local Directory Sites; no Command Central project detected.
- `git rev-parse --short HEAD`: 257aadc4

## Dependencies / Blocks
- CC-001 must be triaged/fixed or captured as the first dogfood target.
- CC-003 must expose the grouped view.
- CC-005 must define the conductor behavior to test.

## Out Of Scope
- Superseded findings are context only and must not be re-opened by this issue.

<!-- openclaw-linear-intake:v1 -->
dependencies: CC-001 must be triaged/fixed or captured as the first dogfood target.
CC-003 must expose the grouped view.
CC-005 must define the conductor behavior to test.
